description: PR-11c cram-sheet data-model + render review (companion to pr11c-cram-sheets.md).
method: operator self-audit of cc_spine/cram_sheet.py + cram_sheet_view.py plus a test suite, THEN an INDEPENDENT agent code review dispatched and COMPLETED before commit per governance §16.0. The reviewer walked learner_state.replay's exact holdout-rejection code order, re-derived every sort key by hand, grepped cram_sheet.py for any answer-key-store import, checked the sibling test suite's CSS-class-vs-element trap for recurrence, and probed the Makefile/CI gate patterns for coverage gaps.
independent_verdict: accept_with_fixes (1 major + 3 minor; all fixed before commit)

independent_findings.problem: _domain_accuracy defaulted to 0.0 whenever a domain had no per_domain entry. learner_state.readiness_report explicitly excludes the exam_strategy bank from per_domain, and every exam_strategy item carries domain 'global' -- so a missed exam_strategy item produced a 'global' group with accuracy None/0 attempts in the JSON, yet the SORT treated it as 0% accuracy and pinned it as the single weakest domain, ahead of any genuinely-failing scored domain. Reproduced: a missed global item outranked a real 10%-accuracy D3 domain.
independent_findings.resolution: sort key changed to (dom not in per_domain, accuracy, dom) so any domain absent from per_domain sorts LAST, never first. Regression test test_unscored_domain_sorts_last_not_as_worst uses real goldset item ids (D1.1.1.definition.0001, D3.3.1.definition.0001, global.0.0.strategy.0001) and asserts the ordering is [D3, D1, global], with global's accuracy staying None (not fabricated).

independent_findings.location: cram_sheet.py:build_cram_sheet (domain assignment for an unresolved item_id)
independent_findings.problem: when a journal entry's item_id did not resolve via quiz.load_goldset() (e.g. a persisted attempt stream replayed against an updated goldset), domain was set to Python None -- schema-invalid against the enum-typed 'domain' field, and a hard crash in cram_sheet_view.render_html (html.escape(None) raises AttributeError).
independent_findings.resolution: added an explicit _UNKNOWN_DOMAIN = 'unknown' sentinel, used whenever the item does not resolve; added 'unknown' to the schema's domain enum. Regression test test_unresolved_item_id_falls_back_to_unknown_domain_not_none proves both the JSON shape (domain == 'unknown', a str) and that render_html no longer crashes on it.

independent_findings.problem: priority is read from the ITEM's current aggregate confidence flag in learner_state, not attributed per-misconception. If a single item ever mapped two distinct misconceptions to different distractors (the registry/key format permits this; no current goldset item does), two open journal entries on that item would share one priority value even if only one underlying miss was actually overconfident.
independent_findings.resolution: not reachable with current content (verified: no answer-keys.json entry maps distractors to more than one distinct misconception_id). Explicitly documented as an accepted limitation in cram_sheet.py's module docstring and this report, rather than re-plumbing learner_state.replay's journal schema (an already-shipped, independently-reviewed module from PR-9/9.1) to carry a per-attempt confidence flag for a currently-unreachable case.

independent_findings.location: Makefile: dashboard-html-check / cram-sheet-html-check; .github/workflows/cc-spine.yml: the two corresponding steps
independent_findings.problem: the HTML leakage gates (for both the dashboard and the new cram sheet) checked a narrower forbidden-field list than their JSON-gate siblings, missing choices/answer_key_hash/item_hash/fact_ids/expected_keywords. Currently dormant (neither render function reads those fields today) but an inconsistency between two gates guarding the same answer-key-isolation boundary.
independent_findings.resolution: widened all four HTML gate patterns (dashboard + cram-sheet, Makefile + workflow) to the same compound-identifier set as their JSON gates: correct_index, answer_key_ref, answer_key_hash, item_hash, rationale_refs, distractor_misconceptions, selected_index, choices, fact_ids, expected_keywords. A first attempt also added bare English words (answer, correct, stem, trap, correction, one_sentence_rule) to match the dashboard JSON gate's exact list -- this was caught and reverted before commit: it false-positived against the dashboard's own legitimate static copy ('Wrong answers' section title, 'no answers, no PII' footer reassurance) and even CSS (the -apple-system font-family value contains the substring 'stem'). Verified empirically against real generated HTML for both pages before landing the safe, narrower, compound-identifier-only pattern.

clean_areas_confirmed_by_reviewer: holdout rejection in learner_state.replay is the FIRST check in the per-attempt loop -- before item/key lookup, before grading, before any items/journal state is created -- confirmed by reading the exact code order; a holdout item_id can never reach the journal cram_sheet.py reads

cram_sheet.py imports only quiz (never learner_state, never an answer-key/holdout module) and calls only quiz.load_goldset() -- confirmed by grep; a structurally stronger isolation guarantee than 'the journal happens to be clean'

_entry() builds an explicit 10-key literal dict, no **item/**journal_row spread anywhere -- choices/correct_index/answer_key_ref/rationale_refs/distractor_misconceptions/hashes/selected_index are structurally absent, not merely filtered
cram_sheet_view.py never reads an item dict at all; every render function only touches the pre-curated entry dict
the priority-then-recency-then-id sort key and the closed-recap max-recorded_at dedup were both re-derived independently and confirmed correct

the 26-test suite deliberately avoided the CSS-class-vs-rendered-element false-positive pattern already caught twice in the sibling dashboard-view suite (verified by grepping _STYLE for every asserted string)
HTML escaping is applied consistently to every content-derived string field
note: Independent review completed BEFORE this commit (the PR-9 lesson) and returned accept-with-fixes with one MAJOR finding -- an ordering bug that could permanently mis-rank a learner's weakest domain. Caught pre-commit, not shipped. All four findings applied (three fixed in code, one explicitly documented as an accepted, currently-unreachable limitation) before commit.
